skill.name: agile-planning
skill.description: Generate structured agile release plans with sprint schedules, ticket codes, roadmaps, milestones, dependencies, and planning guidance.
skill.summary: Create agile release plans with coded sprints, tasks, milestones, and roadmap views.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown planning examples rather than executable code. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md, references/sprint-guide.md, or references/template.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick fences and inline examples as Ruby or shell execution. These locations are markdown templates and agile planning examples; they do not instruct the agent to run commands or execute user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced markdown examples, template delimiters, or planning text. There is no script file, command invocation, subprocess call, or executable instruction at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Planning Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged ordinary words and ticket examples as weak cryptographic algorithms. The reviewed content discusses descriptions, design, dependencies, sprint codes, and task codes, not cryptographic functions or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are prose or markdown task templates. No hashing, encryption, crypto library use, password handling, or key material appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Avoidance Guidance Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a system reconnaissance pattern at a line that starts an agile planning anti-pattern section. The word appears in normal documentation and does not request system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 74 is a documentation heading for planning mistakes to avoid. There is no command, host enumeration, environment probing, or data collection instruction.

content.user_title: Plan Agile Releases With Structured Sprints
content.value_statement: Release planning can become inconsistent when sprint scope, task IDs, and milestones are scattered. This skill creates clear agile plans with coded sprints, dependencies, roadmap views, and release checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Creates sprint schedules with sequential SPRINT-001 style codes.
Breaks release scope into specific tasks with T-001 style ticket codes.
Builds roadmap sections by month, quarter, or project phase.
Adds milestones such as alpha, beta, feature freeze, and production launch.
Documents dependencies between sprints, tasks, teams, and external inputs.
Applies capacity guidance for one-week, two-week, and three-week sprints.
limitations: Does not connect to Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues, or other planning systems.
Does not calculate team velocity from historical project data automatically.
Needs user-provided scope, team size, timeline, priorities, and constraints.
Produces planning guidance, not binding delivery commitments or budget forecasts.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Release Plan
use_cases.description: Turn feature goals, timeline, and team capacity into coded sprints, task lists, milestones, and a stakeholder roadmap.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sprint Planning Material
use_cases.description: Convert a backlog into sprint themes, measurable tasks, dependencies, and milestone checkpoints before a planning session.

use_cases.title: Communicate Delivery Timeline
use_cases.description: Summarize planned sprint outcomes and release checkpoints in a format that teams and stakeholders can review quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Release Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with core planning details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an agile release plan for [project name]. Timeline is [number] weeks, team size is [number], sprint length is [number] weeks, and must-have features are [list].

prompt_templates.title: Backlog to Sprint Schedule
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using a feature backlog.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this backlog into an agile sprint schedule with SPRINT-001 codes, T-001 task codes, goals, dependencies, and milestones: [paste backlog].

prompt_templates.title: Roadmap With Capacity Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with capacity and stakeholder reporting needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a release roadmap for [project] using [team size], [availability constraints], [target launch date], and [priority levels]. Include risks, milestones, and phase summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Phase Release Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for complex releases across teams or phases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-phase agile release plan for [product]. Separate foundation, feature, integration, testing, and launch phases. Show sprint dependencies, task sequencing, and release checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Plan an eight-week MVP for a two-person mobile app team.
output: Four two-week sprints with SPRINT-001 through SPRINT-004 labels.
Task codes from T-001 onward for user accounts, tracking, charts, and launch preparation.
Milestones for beta testing, app store submission, and public launch.

output_examples.input: Create a six-sprint checkout release roadmap for an ecommerce platform.
output: Sprint themes for payment foundation, checkout UI, PayPal support, testing, beta launch, and production release.
Dependencies that connect payment APIs, checkout screens, QA, beta feedback, and rollout readiness.
A roadmap view that stakeholders can scan by quarter or month.

output_examples.input: Convert a feature backlog into a sprint plan with dependencies.
output: Backlog items grouped into realistic sprint goals.
Sequential task IDs that remain unique across the release.
Dependency notes that show blocked work and required prerequisites.

best_practices: Provide scope, target dates, team size, sprint length, and priority levels before asking for a plan.
Ask for explicit dependencies and risk notes when the work spans teams, vendors, or integrations.
Review generated dates and capacity assumptions against holidays, PTO, and known delivery constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a detailed release plan without sharing project scope or team capacity.
Do not treat generated sprint dates as commitments without team review and estimation.
Do not reuse sprint or task codes across separate planning revisions unless the team confirms continuity.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces agile release plans with sprint codes, task codes, goals, dependencies, roadmap sections, and milestones.

faq.question: Can it work with one-week or three-week sprints?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference guidance covers one-week, two-week, and three-week sprint planning patterns.

faq.question: Does it integrate with project management tools?
faq.answer: No. It creates planning text that users can copy into tools such as Jira, Linear, or GitHub Issues.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide project scope, timeline, team size, sprint duration, key milestones, priorities, and major dependencies.

faq.question: Can it help with stakeholder roadmaps?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize sprint outcomes by month, quarter, or phase for stakeholder review.

faq.question: Is this a security-sensitive skill?
faq.answer: No evidence of executable code, network access, secret handling, or prompt injection was found during review.
